# skvault — The Sovereign Secrets Vault

> A KeePass-backed secrets vault whose master key is PGP-sealed to your sovereign identity and unlocked into gpg-agent. Split out of skingest, depends on capauth.seal, reached through one stable shim. The vault never holds your master. GPL-3.0.

skvault is the sovereign secrets-vault package of the smilinTux / SKWorld ecosystem. It keeps
credentials in a KeePass database whose master passphrase is never stored in plaintext — it is
PGP-sealed to your sovereign identity (via `capauth.seal`) and, while unlocked, held only inside
gpg-agent. `lock` evicts it. Compromise the vault file alone and you have nothing without the key.

skvault was split out of skingest (EPIC 11eeac9e) so secrets management is its own single-purpose
service. The user-facing entry point is a stable bash shim at `~/.skenv/bin/skvault` that delegates
to the `skvault-backend` console script — every consumer calls the shim, never the package, so the
implementation can move by editing one line.

## Usage

```sh
skvault unlock            # unseal the master into gpg-agent (via capauth.seal)
skvault status            # is the vault open?
skvault list git          # list matching credentials
skvault get github --show # fetch a credential
skvault lock              # evict the master from gpg-agent
```

## Command surface

`skvault unlock | lock | status | get <q> | list [filter] | creds-* | vault-*`

- **unlock [--word W]** — unseal the KeePass master into gpg-agent
- **lock** — evict the master from gpg-agent (re-seal)
- **status** (vault-status) — current seal state
- **get <q>** (creds-get) — fetch a credential by query
- **list [filter]** (creds-list) — list credentials
- **creds-init / creds-status** — initialize / inspect the KeePass DB
- **vault-share-init --holders a,b,c [--threshold k]** — split passphrase into k-of-n Shamir shares, each PGP-sealed to a holder
- **vault-recover --providers a,b [--totp CODE]** — reconstruct the passphrase from shares
- **seal-word** — seal the unlock-word blob

## Architecture — where it sits

| Component | Role |
|-----------|------|
| **capauth** | crypto home — identity, sign/verify, and the `seal/unseal` primitive |
| **skvault** | secrets home — KeePass vault + seal lifecycle + recovery + TOTP (DEPENDS ON `capauth.seal`) |
| **skingest** | pure ingestion — the vault's former home; now also seals via `capauth.seal` |
| **Consumers** | `skos.secrets`, skguide, `.claude` hooks — all reach skvault through the shim |

Flow: **capauth (seal) → skvault (keep) → skos / skguide / hooks (consume)**.
Sibling single-purpose services: skingest (ingestion), skcomms (transport), skchat (chat).

## The fixins — the engineering story

- **Split out of skingest** — secrets management and document ingestion are different jobs; the vault became its own package.
- **Depends on capauth.seal** — crypto has one home (capauth); skvault reuses its seal/unseal rather than reimplementing it.
- **The stable shim seam** — `~/.skenv/bin/skvault` decouples every caller from where the backend lives; migration edits one line.
- **Never holds the master** — the KeePass master is sealed to your PGP key and lives only in gpg-agent while open; the vault file alone is worthless.

## Components

- **vault_creds** — KeePass credential store/get/list
- **vault** — gpg-agent seal lifecycle (lock/unlock/status)
- **vault_recovery + shamir** — k-of-n sovereign social recovery (HashiCorp-Vault model, Shamir over GF(256))
- **totp** — RFC 6238 second-factor gate on sensitive actions

## About smilinTux

skvault is part of the **smilinTux sovereign AI ecosystem** — a suite of open-source tools built
by King Divad (Chef/David) and Lumina to give humans and AI agents genuine, authentic
infrastructure they can own and trust. No corporate clouds. No black-box vendors. GPL-3.0 forever.
